Interface Reference

All Superinterfaces:
EqualsModProperty<SourceElement>, ModelElement, ProgramElement, SourceElement, SyntaxElement
All Known Subinterfaces:
ConstructorReference, IExecutionContext, MemberReference, MethodOrConstructorReference, NameReference, ProgramConstruct, TypeReference, TypeReferenceInfix
All Known Implementing Classes:
ArrayLength, ArrayLengthReference, ArrayPostDecl, ArrayReference, Break, ConstructorCall, Continue, CreateObject, DoBreak, EnhancedForElimination, EvaluateArgs, ExecutionContext, ExpandMethodBody, FieldReference, ForInitUnfoldTransformer, ForToWhile, InitArray, InitArrayCreation, IsStatic, LabelJumpStatement, MetaClassReference, MethodCall, MethodReference, MultipleVarDecl, New, NewArray, PackageReference, PostWork, ProgramSV, ProgramTransformer, ReattachLoopInvariant, SchematicFieldReference, SchemaTypeReference, SpecialConstructorCall, SpecialConstructorReference, StaticInitialisation, SuperConstructorReference, SuperReference, SwitchToIf, ThisConstructorReference, ThisReference, TypeOf, TypeRef, TypeReferenceImp, Unpack, UnwindLoop, VariableReference
public interface Reference extends ProgramElement
References are uses of names, variables or members. They can have a name (such as TypeReferences) or be anonymous (such as ArrayReference). taken from COMPOST and changed to achieve an immutable structure
